content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

Both amendments are valid and confirmed by codebase inspection. The git diff between fa1bae34 and HEAD shows zero Rust source file changes — only metadata/project tracking files were modified. codex_model_and_reasoning_effort() at process_backend.rs:1319 only handles reasoning effort suffixes (-xhigh/-high/-medium/-low) with no -fast support. Neither codex_new_session_args() nor codex_resume_args() have any service_tier or fast-mode wiring. No 'fast' references exist in process_backend_test.rs either.

## Votes

- `fr-2-54112b84`: Accept
  Verified: codex_model_and_reasoning_effort() at line 1319 only iterates over ["xhigh", "high", "medium", "low"] suffixes. A model ID like 'gpt-5.4-xhigh-fast' would not match any of these (since '-fast' is the actual suffix, not '-low' etc.), so it would pass through as-is with no reasoning effort extracted and no fast flag set. Both codex_new_session_args() and codex_resume_args() destructure only (base_model_id, reasoning_effort) with no fast-mode parameter. Grepping for 'fast' in process_backend.rs returns zero matches. The feature is genuinely unimplemented.
- `fr-2-acc9174e`: Accept
  Verified: git diff fa1bae34..HEAD --stat shows only 5 files changed, all of which are metadata/tracking files (.beads/issues.jsonl, ralph-burning project artifacts, result). Zero .rs source files were modified. Grepping for 'fast' in process_backend_test.rs returns no matches — none of the claimed tests exist. The plan_and_implement stage produced an artifact claiming completion but performed no actual code changes. The implementation must be done from scratch.
